_comment: Single source of truth for CI-plane secret rotation (scripts/rotate_secret.py). Each entry is one rotatable secret and lists every place its value lives: GitHub Actions ENVIRONMENT secrets (repo + environment + secret_name) and/or non-Terraform Vercel project env vars (project_id + env_key). SCOPE: CI-plane credentials ONLY (build/deploy plumbing). App secrets in Terraform's SECRETS_INVENTORY (DB URLs, RSA keypair, KDF_SERVICE_KEY, STRIPE_*, OIDC client secrets, CRON_SECRET, etc.) are Terraform-owned — do NOT add them here; a direct write would drift. If one is ever added, set "terraform_managed": true and the engine refuses to write it and points to the terraform SECRETS_ROTATION runbook. 'kind' is the intended primary mode (paste|generate); 'generator' picks the generate strategy (vercel_token|random_urlsafe); 'per_env' makes generate mint a distinct value per environment. Vercel entries whose project_id starts with 'TODO' are skipped with a warning.

secrets.name: GH_PACKAGES_PAT
secrets.description: Fine-grained PAT (contents:read on the private SDK repo) used at build time to pip-install kdf-auth-sdk. GitHub cannot mint PATs via API, so this is paste-only: owner creates the PAT, stages it in the SECRET_VALUE_NEW repo secret, then runs paste.

secrets.name: VERCEL_TOKEN
secrets.description: Per-app Vercel API token the app's CI uses to deploy. Auto-minted via the Vercel API (one unique token per target), written to that repo's GitHub environment secret, old token deleted. The 'app' field drives the --apps filter.
